title: JanusMesh: Fast and Zero-Shot 3D Visual Illusion Generation via Cross-Space Denoising
figure: First fast and zero-shot framework for generating 3D visual illusions via cross-space denoising.
abstract: This paper presents JanusMesh, a fast framework for generating text-driven 3D visual illusions. It utilizes a dual-branch denoising process for voxel space decoding and SDF blending, followed by a view-conditioned texture synthesis module. The method achieves high realism and efficiency, outperforming existing techniques in geometric integrity and semantic recognizability.

title: TriFlow: Generating Artist-Like 3D Mesh Topology via Nearest-Vertex Vector Fields
figure: First generative approach for producing compact 3D meshes with artist-like topology via nearest-vertex vector fields.
abstract: TriFlow introduces a generative method for creating compact 3D meshes with artist-like triangle topology from input geometry conditions like signed distance fields. It utilizes a nearest-vertex vector field to represent mesh topology and employs a latent flow-matching model for synthesis. The approach enhances topology quality and generalization, achieving significant improvements over existing me

title: PointDiffusion: Diffusion-Based Scene Completion in the Point Cloud Domain
figure: First stable scene-scale LiDAR completion model via multi-token Gaussian VAE and single-step diffusion.
abstract: PointDiffusion addresses the challenge of reconstructing dense 3D scenes from sparse LiDAR point clouds using a novel multi-token Gaussian VAE with cross-attention pooling. This approach enables a single-step diffusion completion model that significantly reduces inference latency and improves accuracy, surpassing existing methods in performance.

title: MUSE: Agentic 3D Scene Authoring via Memory-Grounded Incremental Requirement Satisfaction
figure: First memory-grounded framework for controllable 3D scene authoring via incremental requirement satisfaction.
abstract: MUSE is a memory-grounded multi-agent framework for text-driven 3D scene generation that allows for controllable authoring and editing of scenes. It improves success rates in construction and editing tasks while preserving existing content, addressing the limitations of current methods that often require full regeneration or manual intervention.

title: World Tracing: Generative Pixel-Aligned Geometry Beyond the Visible
figure: First generative pixel-aligned geometry representation for 3D point prediction via diffusion.
abstract: World Tracing introduces a generative pixel-aligned geometry representation that predicts 3D points aligned with input pixels while generating geometry beyond the visible surface. It employs a diffusion transformer, WT-DiT, to achieve strong performance in visible-surface reconstruction and complete geometry generation, enabling various applications including text-driven 3D scene editing.

title: ISAP-3D: Identity-Slot Aligned Part-Aware 3D Generation
figure: First identity-slot aligned framework for stable part-aware 3D generation via one-to-one layout prediction.
abstract: ISAP-3D introduces a framework for part-aware 3D generation that addresses structural ambiguity by aligning part identities with generation slots. It enables stable generation through identity-conditioned layout prediction and geometry synthesis, supported by a new part-level dataset. The method shows improved stability and robustness compared to existing approaches.

title: TextHOI-3D: Text-to-3D Hand-Object Interaction via Discrete Multi-View Generation and Joint Mesh Optimization
figure: First text-to-3D framework for hand-object interaction via multi-view generation and joint mesh optimization
abstract: TextHOI-3D is a framework for generating hand-object meshes from text, addressing challenges in preserving semantics, consistency, and geometry. It utilizes a multi-view approach with a CLIP-conditioned autoregressive model to predict visual tokens, leading to improved mesh recovery through joint optimization. Evaluations show significant reductions in object distance and penetration volume compar

title: ABot-Earth 0.5: Generative 3D Earth Model
figure: First generative model for synthesizing 3D environments from satellite imagery via 3D Gaussian Splatting.
abstract: ABot-Earth 0.5 is a generative framework that synthesizes seamless 3D environments from satellite imagery using a novel 3D Gaussian Splatting model. It generates realistic geometry and textures, enabling rapid scene synthesis and real-time visualization, while addressing the sim-to-real domain gap for applications like UAV navigation.

title: EditSSC: Toward Editable Semantic Occupancy Scenes with Unconditional Diffusion Models
figure: First method for editable 3D semantic scene generation using unconditional diffusion models.
abstract: EditSSC introduces a method for 3D semantic scene generation that utilizes 2D Bird's Eye View representations and latent diffusion networks. It allows for editing capabilities without retraining, outperforming existing baselines on unconditional generation tasks. The approach demonstrates the effectiveness of repurposing 2D architectures for 3D scene generation and editing.

title: CP4D: Compositional Physics-aware 4D Scene Generation
figure: First 4D scene generation that integrates static 3D environments with dynamic objects via physical dynamics.
abstract: CP4D introduces a novel approach for generating photorealistic 4D scenes by combining static 3D environments with dynamic, physically grounded objects. The framework employs a three-stage pipeline that includes generating high-fidelity 3D representations, synthesizing realistic object trajectories, and composing coherent 4D scenes. Experiments show significant improvements in visual fidelity and p

title: Rethinking 3D Shape Generation: Diffusion over Superquadrics
figure: First diffusion model that generates 3D shapes using superquadrics for improved efficiency.
abstract: This paper presents a novel approach to 3D shape generation using diffusion models over compact geometric primitives called superquadrics. By reducing the dimensionality of the diffusion state, the method enhances scalability and supports various capabilities, including part-level editing and efficient generation, while maintaining competitive performance on standard benchmarks.

title: WaveDiT: Distribution-Aware Wavelet Flow Matching for Efficient 3D Brain MRI Synthesis
figure: First 3D brain MRI synthesis method using conditional flow matching in wavelet space.
abstract: WaveDiT introduces a conditional flow matching framework for efficient full-resolution 3D brain MRI synthesis, addressing computational challenges in generative augmentation. By leveraging wavelet transforms and adaptive precision modeling, it enhances the alignment of generated MRI data with real distributions, improving anatomical detail and downstream tasks. The method is optimized for single G

title: 3DMorph: Single-Image-Guided Local 3D Shape Editing and Morphing
figure: First training-free framework for single-image-guided local 3D shape editing and morphing.
abstract: 3DMorph addresses the challenge of intuitive local shape editing in 3D by allowing users to modify shapes based on a single edited image. The method localizes the relevant 3D region and applies 2D modifications while preserving unmodified areas, enabling intermediate shape generation for design exploration. A new benchmark, Delta3D, is introduced to evaluate editing quality.

title: GuideCAD: A Lightweight Multimodal Framework for 3D CAD Model Generation via Prefix Embedding
figure: First lightweight multimodal framework for 3D CAD model generation via prefix embedding.
abstract: GuideCAD is a multimodal framework designed for efficient 3D CAD model generation, utilizing a mapping network to convert image embeddings into prefix embeddings. This allows a pretrained language model to integrate visual and textual information, enabling a transformer-based decoder to predict construction sequences for generating 3D CAD models. The method shows high-quality results with fewer pa

title: ARAPDiffusion: ARAP Regularization for Diffusion-Based Deformable Shape Space Learning
figure: First latent diffusion model that incorporates ARAP regularization for shape generation.
abstract: ARAPDiffusion presents a latent diffusion model that learns a continuous shape space for deformable shapes by integrating ARAP deformation as regularization. This approach enhances the encoder/decoder and the diffusion model, allowing for effective shape generation with limited 3D training data. The method shows improved performance in both unconditional and conditional shape generation tasks.

title: HomeWorld: A Unified Floorplan-to-Furnished Framework for Generating Controllable, Densely Interactive Whole-Home Scenes
figure: First unified framework for generating controllable whole-home scenes via hierarchical decomposition.
abstract: HomeWorld introduces a hierarchical framework for generating interactive whole-home scenes, addressing challenges in indoor scene generation. It utilizes a large dataset for floorplan generation and employs image generation models for furniture layouts, enhancing layout diversity and 3D design appeal. The method includes a VLM-based refiner for object placement and a 3D generative model for asset

title: Global-Local Monte Carlo Tree Search in Vision-Language Models for Text-to-3D Indoor Scene Generation
figure: First text-to-3D indoor scene generation using global-local Monte Carlo tree search with diffusion models.
abstract: This paper addresses the challenge of text-to-3D indoor scene generation using large vision-language models. It proposes a global-local tree search approach to optimize object placement in scenes, leveraging a new dataset for evaluation. The method aims to improve realism in generated 3D scenes by integrating hierarchical scene representation and diffusion models for texture prediction.

title: Controllable Dynamic 3D Shape Generation via 3D Trajectories and Text
figure: First controllable dynamic 3D shape generation via 3D trajectories and text.
abstract: T2Mo is a feed-forward framework for generating dynamic 3D shapes based on 3D trajectories and text. It addresses the challenges of language ambiguity by using trajectories for spatial guidance, ensuring object motions adhere to specified paths while reflecting text semantics. The method includes a shape-grounded trajectory embedding for robust handling of various trajectory configurations, demons

title: MeshWeaver: Sparse-Voxel-Guided Surface Weaving for Autoregressive Mesh Generation
figure: First autoregressive mesh generation framework that incorporates sparse-voxel guidance for improved geometric fidelity.
abstract: MeshWeaver is an autoregressive framework for mesh generation that addresses limitations in tokenization efficiency and geometry-aware guidance. It predicts the next vertex in a surface weaving process, utilizing a multi-level sparse-voxel encoder to enhance the generative process. This approach allows for high-quality mesh generation with improved geometric fidelity and a state-of-the-art compres

title: MeshFlow: Efficient Artistic Mesh Generation via MeshVAE and Flow-based Diffusion Transformer
figure: First efficient artistic mesh generation method via MeshVAE and Flow-based diffusion.
abstract: MeshFlow introduces a novel method for generating artistic 3D meshes, overcoming limitations of existing auto-regressive models. By utilizing a Variational Autoencoder for continuous vertex representation and a Rectified Flow transformer for parallel generation, it achieves significant speed and accuracy improvements. The method is 18x faster than previous approaches and maintains high quality in

title: SymTRELLIS: Symmetry-Enforced Voxel Latents for 3D Generation
figure: First method to enforce symmetry in flow-based 3D generation via learned linear operators on voxel latents.
abstract: SymTRELLIS introduces a method for enforcing symmetry in flow-based 3D generation, addressing structural requirements often overlooked in single-view models. By approximating latent-space actions of spatial transformations, it allows for symmetry enforcement during generation without retraining the underlying model. The approach significantly reduces symmetry errors while maintaining reconstructio

title: Conditional Latent Diffusion Model with Fourier-based Motion Modelling for Virtual Population Synthesis
figure: First conditional generative model for 3D+t cardiac mesh sequences via Fourier-based motion modelling.
abstract: The paper presents 4D F-MeshLDM, a conditional generative framework for synthesizing virtual populations of anatomies, specifically 3D+t cardiac meshes. It combines a convolutional mesh VAE, a Fourier series for motion parameterization, and a diffusion prior for controllable synthesis. The model outperforms existing methods in anatomical fidelity and maintains clinical functional indices, demonstr

title: HumanNOVA: Photorealistic, Universal and Rapid 3D Human Avatar Modeling from a Single Image
figure: First model that generates photorealistic 3D human avatars from a single image via a feed-forward architecture.
abstract: HumanNOVA introduces a rapid model for generating photorealistic 3D human avatars from a single RGB image. It utilizes a scalable data generation pipeline and a token-conditioned framework for fast inference, achieving robust performance across diverse input conditions. The approach enhances the quantity and diversity of training data, demonstrating significant improvements in avatar modeling.

title: MORPHOS: Autoregressive 4D Generation with Temporal Structured Latents
figure: First autoregressive framework that generates dynamic 3D assets from videos using Temporal Structured Latents.
abstract: MORPHOS is an autoregressive framework that generates dynamic 3D assets from videos using diverse representations like meshes and 3D Gaussians. It introduces Temporal Structured Latents to ensure temporal consistency and handle evolving topologies, achieving state-of-the-art performance in appearance and competitive results in geometry.

title: PhyScene3D: Physically Consistent Interactive 3D Tabletop Scene Generation
figure: First framework for physically consistent interactive 3D scene generation via Human-Mimetic Constructive Process.
abstract: PhyScene3D addresses the challenge of generating physically consistent 3D tabletop scenes for robotic learning. It reformulates scene generation into a sequential process using a Cognitive Topological Reasoning Chain and integrates a differentiable Signed Distance Field with Test-Time Optimization to ensure physical feasibility. The method shows significant improvements in semantic accuracy and co

title: Learning Neural Deformation Representation for 4D Dynamic Shape Generation
figure: First method for generating 4D dynamic shapes via a new neural deformation representation.
abstract: This paper addresses the generation of 4D dynamic shapes, focusing on quality and efficiency. It introduces a neural deformation representation that separates motion from shape in 4D occupancy fields, improving temporal consistency and rendering speed. The method employs a diffusion model for both unconditional and conditional generation, outperforming previous approaches in 4D dynamic shape gener

title: VolFill: Single-View Amodal 3D Scene Reconstruction with Volumetric Flow Matching
figure: First generative framework for single-view amodal 3D scene reconstruction via hybrid 3D VAE and latent Diffusion Transformer.
abstract: VolFill is a generative framework that reconstructs complete 3D scene geometry from a single RGB image, addressing challenges in inferring hidden structures. It employs a hybrid 3D VAE and a latent Diffusion Transformer to denoise a compact latent representation, enabling robust spatial reasoning and direct surface extraction. Experiments show significant improvements over existing methods.

title: PhyGenHOI: Physically-Aware 4D Generation of Dynamic Human-Object Interactions
figure: First framework for generating physically accurate 4D human-object interactions via motion diffusion and physical simulation.
abstract: PhyGenHOI introduces a framework for generating dynamic human-object interactions in 4D, using 3D Gaussian Splats to represent the entities. It combines generative human motion with physical object simulation, ensuring interactions are physically consistent. The method outperforms existing baselines in generating diverse actions and interactions. A project page is available for further exploration

title: SAM3D-Phys: Towards Multi-Object Interactive Simulation in Real World
figure: First framework that integrates scene reconstruction with generative 3D priors to recover physically simulatable objects.
abstract: SAM3D-Phys addresses the challenge of recovering complete object geometry from reconstructed scenes for physics-based interaction. It combines multi-view reconstruction with generative 3D priors to infer complete object shapes from partial observations, ensuring consistency with the scene. The framework enables interactive simulation of multiple objects in a reconstructed environment.

title: SuperVoxelGPT: Adaptive and Ordered 3D Tokenization for Autoregressive Shape Generation
figure: First adaptive and ordered 3D tokenization for autoregressive shape generation via supervoxel representation.
abstract: SuperVoxelGPT introduces a novel framework for 3D shape generation that utilizes adaptive and ordered supervoxel tokenization to improve the efficiency and quality of autoregressive models. By addressing the limitations of existing tokenization methods, it achieves significant reductions in token sequence length and enhances generation speed and quality.

title: GAP3D: Generative Alignment of VLM Latents to Patch-Level Embeddings for 3D Generation
figure: First modular diffusion-based approach aligning VLM latents to patch-level embeddings for 3D generation.
abstract: GAP3D introduces a diffusion-based method that aligns vision-language model latents to patch-level features for 3D asset generation. It allows a frozen generative model to utilize VLMs as prompt encoders while maintaining spatial structure, enabling effective 3D generation from general-domain image-text pairs and demonstrating zero-shot capabilities.

title: Garment Particles: A 2D--3D Symmetric Garment Representation for Generation and Editing
figure: First framework that jointly encodes 2D sewing patterns and 3D geometry for garment generation and editing via diffusion.
abstract: Garment Particles introduces a 5D point-cloud representation that integrates 2D sewing patterns with 3D geometry, enabling intuitive garment generation from high-level inputs and complex editing operations. The framework supports various generation and editing tasks, achieving state-of-the-art results in garment design. The project website is available for further details.

title: Helix4D: Complex 4D Mesh Generation
figure: First dynamic mesh generation framework for video-conditioned 4D generation via Trellis2 adaptation
abstract: Helix4D is a dynamic mesh generation framework that enhances the Trellis2 model for video-conditioned 4D generation. It addresses challenges in complex topology changes and transparent materials by implementing a sliding-window cross-frame attention mechanism and a novel 4D temporal encoding. Extensive experiments demonstrate its effectiveness in generating high-quality dynamic meshes.

title: F-RNG: Feed-Forward Relightable Neural Gaussians
figure: First feed-forward framework that generates relightable 3D Gaussian splatting assets from sparse-view inputs.
abstract: F-RNG is a feed-forward framework that generates relightable 3D Gaussian splatting assets from sparse-view inputs, enhancing geometry representation and incorporating priors for improved relighting. It achieves faster and higher quality relighting compared to existing methods without requiring retraining of underlying models.

title: BrickAnything: Geometry-Conditioned Buildable Brick Generation with Structure-Aware Tokenization
figure: First geometry-conditioned framework for generating buildable brick structures via autoregressive modeling.
abstract: BrickAnything introduces a geometry-conditioned autoregressive framework for generating physically buildable brick structures from diverse 3D representations. It utilizes point clouds and a structure-aware tree tokenization to ensure the generated sequences adhere to assembly constraints, enhancing stability and geometric fidelity. The method shows improved buildability and consistency with physic

title: Multi-view Consistent 3D Gaussian Head Avatars 'without' Multi-view Generation
figure: First method to generate 3D Gaussian head avatars from 2D images without multi-view data.
abstract: MVCHead introduces a novel approach for generating high-fidelity 3D Gaussian head avatars using only randomly sampled 2D images. It employs a Hierarchical State Space model to ensure multi-view consistency and achieves state-of-the-art results in texture and geometric consistency. The paper also presents FaceGS-10K, a large dataset for training and evaluating 3D head models.

title: Fishbone: From One 3D Asset to a Million Controllable Edits
figure: First unified rib-spine representation for controllable parametric mesh deformation.
abstract: Fishbone introduces a rib-spine representation for generating diverse 3D assets through controllable mesh deformation. It allows real-time adjustments to local and global shape features, enhancing applications in interactive content creation and robot learning. The framework demonstrates efficiency and versatility in 3D generation and editing.

title: AnySurf: Any Surface Generation with Directed Edge
figure: First unified framework for generating open, closed, and hybrid 3D surfaces with accurate face orientation via directed-edge representation.
abstract: AnySurf is a framework designed for generating various types of 3D surfaces, including open and closed forms, with a focus on accurate face orientation. It addresses limitations in existing methods by utilizing a directed-edge enhanced representation and proposes new training techniques. The framework aims to improve the quality of generated meshes for practical applications in industries like gar

title: COSY: Compositional 3DGS Synthesis for Disentangled Human Head Editing
figure: First method for compositional 3DGS synthesis enabling disentangled human head editing via independent generators.
abstract: COSY introduces a novel generator architecture for 3D Gaussian Splatting GANs that allows for independent synthesis of components like hair and skin, facilitating precise editing of human head attributes without requiring segmentation masks. This method enhances disentanglement and visual quality in 3D model generation and editing.

title: GenRecon: Bridging Generative Priors for Multi-View 3D Scene Reconstruction
figure: First approach that combines generative priors with multi-view 3D scene reconstruction via conditional generation.
abstract: GenRecon presents a novel method for high-fidelity 3D scene reconstruction from multi-view RGB images by integrating generative 3D priors. It enables conditional 3D generation over overlapping scene chunks, leveraging the fidelity of existing generative models like Trellis.2. The method produces editable PBR mesh reconstructions, achieving superior results compared to traditional reconstruction te

title: AssetGen: Deployable 3D Asset Generation at Interactive Speed
figure: First 3D generator focusing on deployability and user experience via a coarse-to-refine VecSet framework.
abstract: AssetGen is a 3D generator that produces high-quality meshes and textures from a single reference image in under 30 seconds, emphasizing user experience and deployability. It utilizes a coarse-to-refine framework for geometry generation and supports real-time rendering, achieving competitive visual quality against commercial solutions.

title: PhysX-Omni: Unified Simulation-Ready Physical 3D Generation for Rigid, Deformable, and Articulated Objects
figure: First unified framework for simulation-ready physical 3D generation across diverse asset types.
abstract: PhysX-Omni introduces a unified framework for generating simulation-ready physical 3D assets, addressing limitations of existing methods. It features a novel geometry representation for Vision-Language Models and the PhysXVerse dataset for diverse asset types. The framework shows strong performance in generation and understanding, with potential applications in embodied AI and physics-based simula

title: ROAR-3D: Routing Arbitrary Views for High-Fidelity 3D Generation
figure: First method to upgrade single-view models for arbitrary multi-view 3D generation via a token-wise view router.
abstract: ROAR-3D enhances single-image-to-3D generative models by allowing them to accept multiple unposed images, improving 3D generation quality. It uses a token-wise view router to establish 2D-to-3D correspondences and a dual-stream attention design to maintain primary-view behavior while enriching geometry. This approach achieves state-of-the-art results with minimal additional parameters.

title: PiG-Avatar: Hierarchical Neural-Field-Guided Gaussian Avatars
figure: First Gaussian avatar method that decouples representation from template topology via a continuous neural field.
abstract: PiG-Avatar introduces a novel approach to Gaussian avatars by using a parametric body model for kinematic transport while representing the avatar in a volumetric canonical space. This method allows for the capture of complex clothing geometry and layered surfaces, achieving high-fidelity outputs and state-of-the-art rendering quality across various detail levels.

title: Structural Energy Guidance for View-Consistent Text-to-3D Generation
figure: First method to improve multi-view consistency in text-to-3D generation via structural energy guidance.
abstract: This paper addresses the Janus problem in text-to-3D generation by proposing Structural Energy-Guided Sampling (SEGS), which enhances multi-view consistency without retraining. By constructing a structural energy in the PCA subspace of U-Net features, SEGS integrates into existing pipelines to reduce viewpoint artifacts while maintaining appearance fidelity, demonstrating improved performance acro

title: Efficient 3D Content Reconstruction and Generation
figure: First multi-view diffusion method that generates high-quality 3D assets from text or images in 5-20 seconds.
abstract: This thesis presents Instant3D, a method for automatic 3D content creation that synthesizes assets from text or images using multi-view diffusion and feed-forward techniques. It aims to streamline 3D asset generation for applications in gaming, VR, and robotics, while also addressing 3D reconstruction with a fast pipeline called FastMap.

title: DreamEdit3D: Personalization of Multi-View Diffusion Models for 3D Editing
figure: First method for personalized text-guided 3D editing via multi-view diffusion models
abstract: DreamEdit3D introduces a novel method for personalizing 3D editing using text guidance, addressing challenges in multi-view consistency and spatial control. It utilizes a two-phase optimization strategy to create distinct token embeddings for object components, enabling high-fidelity textured 3D mesh generation from edited prompts.

title: QuadLink: Autoregressive Quad-Dominant Mesh Generation via Point-Relation Learning
figure: First quad-dominant mesh generation method via point-relation learning.
abstract: QuadLink is a framework for generating quad-dominant meshes from point clouds, addressing the limitations of existing methods that produce only triangular or isotropic meshes. It employs a centroid-conditioned vertex linking model to create efficient, sparse, and anisotropic meshes while supporting hybrid polygonal topology. The method shows improved geometric fidelity and topological quality in e

title: EVA01: Unified Native 3D Understanding and Generation via Mixture-of-Transformers
figure: First unified framework for native 3D understanding and generation via Mixture-of-Transformers.
abstract: EVA01 integrates 3D meshes into Multimodal Large Language Models, enabling native understanding and generation. It features a Mixture-of-Transformers architecture that separates understanding and generation, allowing for high-fidelity text-to-3D generation and robust geometric editing. The framework enhances the alignment of semantic and geometric spaces, providing insights for 3D-native multimoda

title: Sat3DGen: Comprehensive Street-Level 3D Scene Generation from Single Satellite Image
figure: First geometry-first methodology for street-level 3D scene generation from satellite images via feed-forward models
abstract: Sat3DGen addresses the challenges of generating street-level 3D scenes from satellite images by introducing a geometry-first methodology. This approach integrates geometric constraints with perspective-view training, significantly improving both 3D accuracy and photorealism. The method demonstrates versatility in various applications, including semantic-map-to-3D synthesis and large-scale meshing.

title: TOPOS: High-Fidelity and Efficient Industry-Grade 3D Head Generation
figure: First framework for single image conditioned 3D head generation with fixed topology via TOPOS-VAE.
abstract: TOPOS is a framework for generating high-fidelity 3D head meshes from a single image, ensuring a consistent studio-style topology. It utilizes a novel variational autoencoder structure and a rectified flow transformer to produce meshes and aligned UV texture maps, achieving state-of-the-art performance in 3D head generation.

title: R-DMesh: Video-Guided 3D Animation via Rectified Dynamic Mesh Flow
figure: First framework that generates high-fidelity 4D meshes rectified to align with video context via a novel VAE.
abstract: R-DMesh addresses the pose misalignment issue in video-guided 3D animation by generating high-fidelity 4D meshes that align with video context. It introduces a VAE that disentangles input into a base mesh, motion trajectories, and a rectification offset, ensuring physical consistency during animation. The method employs a Rectified Flow-based Diffusion Transformer, leveraging a large dataset of dy

title: SynVA: A Modular Toolkit for Vessel Generation and Aneurysm Editing
figure: First toolkit for vascular mesh generation and aneurysm editing via flow-matching and learning-based methods
abstract: SynVA is a modular toolkit designed for generating vascular meshes and anatomically consistent aneurysms. It combines flow-matching techniques with learning-based approaches to create realistic vessel geometries and aneurysms based on existing vascular structures. The toolkit aims to facilitate the generation of large-scale datasets for medical applications, addressing the challenges posed by limi

title: Rigel3D: Rig-aware Latents for Animation-Ready 3D Asset Generation
figure: First method to jointly model geometry and rig structure for animation-ready 3D asset generation via a rig-aware autoencoder.
abstract: Rigel3D introduces a generative approach for creating animation-ready 3D assets that include rigged meshes. It models geometry and rig structure together, allowing for the synthesis of both surface and skeleton representations conditioned on images. This method enhances the usability of 3D assets in animation workflows, outperforming existing rigging techniques.

title: GTA: Advancing Image-to-3D World Generation via Geometry Then Appearance Video Diffusion
figure: First image-to-3D world generation method that follows a Geometry-Then-Appearance paradigm via video diffusion.
abstract: GTA is a novel image-to-3D world generation method that improves structural fidelity by generating coarse geometric structures followed by fine-grained appearance synthesis. It addresses limitations in existing approaches and enhances cross-view consistency, demonstrating superior performance in fidelity and visual quality. The method is versatile and applicable to various downstream tasks.

title: Optimizing 4D Wires for Sparse 3D Abstraction
figure: First unified framework for 3D geometric abstraction using a continuous 4D wire parameterized as a B-spline.
abstract: This paper introduces a framework for 3D geometric abstraction using a continuous 4D wire, enhancing structural coherence and aesthetic quality. By employing a differentiable rendering pipeline and modern optimization techniques, the method supports applications like image-to-3D abstraction and multi-view generation, outperforming traditional discrete curve approaches.

title: PG-3DGS: Optimizing 3D Gaussian Splatting to Satisfy Physics Objectives
figure: First framework that combines physics simulation with 3D Gaussian representations for generating physically functional 3D structures.
abstract: PG-3DGS introduces a method that integrates differentiable physics simulation with 3D Gaussian representations to generate geometries that are visually accurate and physically functional. The approach optimizes shapes based on physical objectives, resulting in objects that exhibit meaningful behaviors, such as teapots that pour and airplanes that generate lift. Experiments demonstrate improved phy

title: Pixal3D: Pixel-Aligned 3D Generation from Images
figure: First pixel-aligned 3D generation from images via a novel conditioning scheme.
abstract: Pixal3D introduces a pixel-aligned 3D generation paradigm that enhances fidelity in image-to-3D synthesis. By establishing direct pixel-to-3D correspondence, it improves the quality of generated 3D assets and extends to multi-view generation. The method shows significant advancements in producing high-fidelity 3D scenes from images.
